question: How hot does the surface of Mercury get?
expected_answer: The planet Mercury's daytime surface reaches about 430 degrees Celsius, roughly 800 degrees Fahrenheit.
notes: Accept 420-450 C. Must be about the planet, not the element or anything else. The model plausibly knows this from memory, so search is optional; if it searches, the right article is the planet.
answer: The surface of Mercury reaches about **430 °C (800 °F)** at its hottest, on the daytime side facing the Sun. Because Mercury has essentially no atmosphere to retain heat, temperatures swing dramatically—dropping to around **−180 °C (−290 °F)** on the night side. This gives it one of the largest surface temperature ranges of any planet in the Solar System.
reason: Answer states 430°C (800°F) for daytime surface, matching the expected value and within the accepted 420-450C tolerance; no search was needed per case notes.
